title: Exam Guide + Bedrock Foundations
learningObjectives: Understand the AIP-C01 exam structure, domains, and skill weighting
Identify architectural design patterns for GenAI on AWS
Understand when to use Bedrock vs SageMaker vs custom deployment
Learn the InvokeModel, Converse, and streaming APIs
Apply Well-Architected GenAI Lens principles

tasks.title: Official Exam Guide PDF - Read End to End
tasks.description: Mark every skill that feels unfamiliar. This is your roadmap for the next 28 days.

tasks.title: Amazon Bedrock User Guide - Getting Started
tasks.description: Covers InvokeModel, InvokeModelWithResponseStream, StartAsyncInvoke, and the Converse API. Understand the request/response format for each.

tasks.title: How to Choose Generative AI on AWS
tasks.description: Service selection decision guide. Critical for exam questions asking 'which service should you use?'

tasks.title: Amazon Bedrock API Reference
tasks.description: Skim the API operations: InvokeModel, InvokeModelWithResponseStream, StartAsyncInvoke, Converse, ConverseStream. Understand input/output schemas.

tasks.title: Getting Started with Amazon Bedrock (AWS Official)
tasks.description: AWS official walkthrough of the Bedrock console, model access, and playground.

handsOn.title: Explore Bedrock Playground with Multiple Models
handsOn.description: Try the Bedrock console playground to invoke different model families and compare outputs.
steps: Open the AWS Console and navigate to Amazon Bedrock
Go to Model access and enable Claude, Nova, and Llama models
Open the Chat playground and select Claude Sonnet
Enter a prompt like 'Explain the Well-Architected Framework for GenAI in 3 bullet points' and note the response
Switch to Amazon Nova Pro and resubmit the same prompt to compare outputs

handsOn.title: Run Bedrock Workshop InvokeModel Notebook
handsOn.description: Clone the Bedrock workshop and run the text generation notebook to practice InvokeModel and Converse APIs.
steps: Clone the amazon-bedrock-workshop repository from GitHub
Open the 01_Generation notebook in SageMaker Studio or locally with Jupyter
Run the InvokeModel example with Claude and observe the request/response format
Run the Converse API example and compare the unified format to InvokeModel
Modify the prompt and temperature parameter to see how outputs change

furtherReading.title: Amazon Bedrock Deep Dive — SimpleAWS Newsletter
furtherReading.description: Comprehensive overview of Bedrock architecture: model access, inference options, agents, guardrails, and pricing tiers.

furtherReading.title: Converse API Guide — Carry Out a Conversation
furtherReading.description: Official guide covering Converse and ConverseStream operations — the unified, model-agnostic API for all Bedrock models.

furtherReading.title: Amazon Bedrock Complete Deep Dive — DEV Community
furtherReading.description: End-to-end Bedrock walkthrough: model access, playground, APIs, Knowledge Bases, Agents, and Guardrails.

furtherReading.title: Well-Architected GenAI Lens
furtherReading.description: Architecture patterns and best practices for GenAI workloads tested in Domain 1.

furtherReading.title: Amazon Bedrock Recipes — All Hands-On Examples
furtherReading.description: Curated recipe portal: Converse API, agents, flows, guardrails, knowledge bases — runnable notebooks.

title: FM Selection, Resilience, and Model Switching
learningObjectives: Compare all Bedrock model families and their modalities
Understand inference parameters (temperature, top-p, max tokens)
Design dynamic model selection with Lambda + AppConfig
Implement resilience with Cross-Region Inference and circuit breakers
Use Step Functions for circuit breaker patterns

tasks.title: Bedrock Supported Models - Model Catalog
tasks.description: Know every model family: Amazon Nova (Micro/Lite/Pro/Premier), Anthropic Claude, Meta Llama, Mistral, Cohere, Stability AI. Know modalities.

tasks.title: Bedrock Inference Parameters
tasks.description: Temperature, top-p, top-k, max tokens, stop sequences. Know what each controls and when to adjust.

tasks.title: Bedrock Cross-Region Inference
tasks.description: Automatic failover to another region when primary is unavailable. Key resilience pattern.

tasks.title: Intelligent Prompt Routing for Cost and Latency Benefits
tasks.description: Auto-analyzes prompt complexity and routes to cheapest capable model. Automated model cascading.

tasks.title: Amazon Bedrock: Simplifying GenAI Development
tasks.description: Overview of model selection, customization, and deployment patterns in Bedrock.

handsOn.title: Compare Model Outputs in Bedrock Playground
handsOn.description: Systematically compare outputs from Claude, Nova, Llama, and Mistral for the same prompt to understand model family strengths.
steps: Open Bedrock Chat playground and select Claude Sonnet
Enter a complex reasoning prompt: 'A company has 3 AWS accounts. Design a least-privilege IAM strategy for Bedrock access across accounts.'
Record the response quality, latency, and token count
Repeat with Amazon Nova Pro, Meta Llama, and Mistral
Compare results in a table: model, quality, tokens, latency

handsOn.title: Test Inference Parameters (Temperature, Top-p)
handsOn.description: Experiment with temperature and top-p settings to understand their effect on model creativity and determinism.
steps: Open Bedrock Chat playground with Claude Sonnet
Set temperature to 0 and enter: 'Write a haiku about cloud computing'
Run the same prompt 3 times and confirm identical outputs (deterministic)
Change temperature to 1.0 and run the same prompt 3 times — observe variation
Test top-p at 0.1 vs 0.9 with the same prompt and note the difference in creativity

handsOn.title: Set Up Cross-Region Inference Profile
handsOn.description: Configure a Cross-Region Inference profile to enable automatic failover for Bedrock model invocations.
steps: Open the Bedrock console and navigate to Cross-Region Inference
Create an inference profile for Claude Sonnet targeting us-east-1 and us-west-2
Note the inference profile ARN
Use the AWS CLI to invoke the model via the inference profile ARN: aws bedrock-runtime invoke-model --model-id <profile-arn>
Verify in CloudWatch that the request was routed to one of the configured regions

furtherReading.title: Bedrock Model Selection — Solution Architect's Guide
furtherReading.description: How to choose the right FM for cost, quality, and latency — decision framework for Bedrock model families.

furtherReading.title: Intelligent Prompt Routing for Cost and Latency
furtherReading.description: Auto-routes to cheapest capable model within a family for up to 30% cost savings.

furtherReading.title: Cross-Region Inference Workshop Notebook
furtherReading.description: Jupyter notebook for setting up and testing Cross-Region Inference profiles.

furtherReading.title: Circuit Breaker with Step Functions and DynamoDB
furtherReading.description: Resilience pattern for FM API calls with automatic failure detection and fallback.

furtherReading.title: Intelligent Prompt Routing — Understanding and Setup
furtherReading.description: Configure prompt routers: auto-route to cheapest capable model within a family.

title: SageMaker for GenAI
learningObjectives: Understand SageMaker endpoint deployment patterns
Know when to use SageMaker JumpStart vs Bedrock
Understand LoRA, QLoRA, and full fine-tuning trade-offs
Use SageMaker Model Registry for version management
Apply SageMaker Clarify for bias detection and Model Monitor for drift

tasks.title: SageMaker JumpStart Foundation Models
tasks.description: How to deploy foundation models via JumpStart. Understand the deployment configuration options.

tasks.title: SageMaker Model Registry
tasks.description: Version management, approval workflows, deployment pipelines for models.

tasks.title: SageMaker Clarify for Bias Detection
tasks.description: Pre-training and post-training bias detection. Model explainability.

tasks.title: SageMaker Ground Truth and Ground Truth Plus
tasks.description: Generating evaluation datasets and human feedback loops for GenAI.

tasks.title: Advanced Fine-Tuning Methods on SageMaker AI
tasks.description: Compares LoRA, QLoRA, and full fine-tuning. Key for exam questions about model customization.

tasks.title: Tutorials Dojo AIP-C01 Exam Guide (SageMaker coverage)
tasks.description: Community exam guide covering SageMaker topics for the certification.

handsOn.title: Deploy a JumpStart Foundation Model Endpoint
handsOn.description: Use SageMaker JumpStart to deploy a foundation model endpoint and test inference.
steps: Open SageMaker Studio and navigate to JumpStart
Search for a foundation model (e.g., Llama 3 8B or Mistral 7B)
Click Deploy and select an ml.g5.2xlarge instance
Wait for the endpoint to reach InService status (5-10 minutes)
Send a test inference request using the Studio notebook or AWS CLI and verify the response

handsOn.title: Explore LoRA Fine-Tuning Configuration on Bedrock
handsOn.description: Walk through the Bedrock fine-tuning console to understand LoRA configuration options.
steps: Open the Bedrock console and navigate to Custom models → Fine-tuning
Select a supported model (e.g., Amazon Nova or Llama) and click Create fine-tuning job
Review the training data format requirements (JSONL with prompt/completion pairs)
Examine the hyperparameter options: epochs, batch size, learning rate, warmup steps
Note the S3 bucket requirements for training data and output artifacts (do not submit unless you have training data ready)

handsOn.title: Review SageMaker Model Registry Workflow
handsOn.description: Explore Model Registry for versioning and approval workflows using the SageMaker console.
steps: Open SageMaker Studio and navigate to Model Registry
Create a new Model Package Group called 'genai-models'
Review the approval status workflow: PendingManualApproval → Approved → Deployed
Note how model versions are tracked with metadata, metrics, and lineage

furtherReading.title: Advanced Fine-Tuning Methods on SageMaker
furtherReading.description: Compares LoRA, QLoRA, and full fine-tuning. Key for exam questions about model customization.

furtherReading.title: Multi-LoRA Serving with vLLM on SageMaker
furtherReading.description: Dozens of LoRA adapters sharing one GPU with hot-swapping.

furtherReading.title: Reinforcement Fine-Tuning — 66% Accuracy Gain
furtherReading.description: 66% average accuracy gain over base models with reinforcement fine-tuning on Bedrock.

furtherReading.title: LLM Fine-Tuning on AWS — SFT, Continued Pre-Training, RLHF
furtherReading.description: Comprehensive SageMaker fine-tuning: SFT, continued pre-training, RLHF — QLoRA, LoRA, full fine-tuning compared.

furtherReading.title: Bedrock Customization Workshop (Fine-Tuning Notebooks)
furtherReading.description: Hands-on model customization: continued pre-training, supervised fine-tuning, evaluation.

title: Data Pipelines + Processing for FM Consumption
learningObjectives: Map AWS services to data types (Comprehend=text, Transcribe=audio, Textract=docs, Rekognition=images)
Design multimodal data processing pipelines
Understand Bedrock Data Automation for automated processing
Use Macie for PII discovery and Lake Formation for data access control
Build Step Functions orchestrations for batch document processing

tasks.title: Amazon Bedrock Data Automation
tasks.description: Automated multimodal data processing pipeline. Key for questions about processing mixed content types.

tasks.title: Amazon Comprehend Documentation
tasks.description: Entity extraction, sentiment analysis, intent detection from text.

tasks.title: Amazon Textract Documentation
tasks.description: OCR, text extraction, table extraction from documents and images.

tasks.title: Amazon Macie for PII Discovery
tasks.description: Scan S3 buckets for PII BEFORE ingesting into Knowledge Bases. Critical pre-processing step.

tasks.title: AWS Lake Formation for Granular Data Access
tasks.description: Column/row/cell-level access control for data feeding GenAI pipelines.

tasks.title: Bedrock Data Automation + Guardrails PII Pipeline
tasks.description: End-to-end PII detection and redaction architecture using BDA and Guardrails.

tasks.title: Amazon Bedrock Agents: Easy Data Pipelines
tasks.description: Video walkthrough of building data pipelines with Bedrock.

handsOn.title: Build a Textract → Comprehend → Bedrock Pipeline
handsOn.description: Create a simple document processing pipeline that extracts text, identifies entities, and generates a summary.
steps: Upload a sample PDF document to an S3 bucket
Use the AWS CLI to call Textract DetectDocumentText and save the extracted text
Pass the extracted text to Comprehend DetectEntities to identify people, organizations, and dates
Send the extracted text and entities to Bedrock InvokeModel (Claude) with a prompt: 'Summarize this document and highlight key entities'
Compare the pipeline output with Bedrock Data Automation's single-API approach

handsOn.title: Test Bedrock Data Automation (BDA) for Document Processing
handsOn.description: Use Bedrock Data Automation to process a document with a single API call instead of chaining services.
steps: Open the Bedrock console and navigate to Data Automation
Create a new project and upload a sample multi-page document
Configure the extraction blueprint to extract key fields
Run the automation and review the structured output
Compare the result with the manual Textract → Comprehend pipeline from the previous activity

furtherReading.title: Intelligent Document Processing at Scale with BDA
furtherReading.description: End-to-end IDP with BDA: classification, extraction, normalization, validation — reusable IaC.

furtherReading.title: IDP with Textract, Bedrock, and LangChain
furtherReading.description: OCR + LLM pipeline: Textract extracts text, Bedrock generates structured output, LangChain orchestrates.

furtherReading.title: BDA Document Processing Samples
furtherReading.description: Sample Bedrock Data Automation pipelines for document processing.

furtherReading.title: Multimodal Power of BDA for Unstructured Data
furtherReading.description: Process documents, images, audio, video — multimodal data to structured output with BDA.

furtherReading.title: Lessons Learned with BDA in an IDP Product
furtherReading.description: Real-world BDA experience: gotchas, workarounds, regional limits.

title: Vector Stores Deep Dive
learningObjectives: Compare all Bedrock KB vector store options (OpenSearch, Aurora pgvector, Neptune, Kendra, DocumentDB)
Understand hybrid search (BM25 + k-NN) and when it matters
Know the 'exam trigger' phrases for each vector store selection
Design vector store architecture for different use cases

tasks.title: Bedrock Knowledge Bases - Vector Store Options
tasks.description: Chunking strategies, vector store options, data source sync. Core reference.

tasks.title: AWS Prescriptive Guidance: Choosing a Vector Database for RAG
tasks.description: Side-by-side comparison of all vector DB options. Essential exam reference.

tasks.title: Hybrid Search in Bedrock Knowledge Bases
tasks.description: 8-12% improvement over keyword-only search. Know when hybrid search is the answer.

tasks.title: OpenSearch Managed vs Serverless for KB

tasks.title: Bedrock RAG Workshop - Knowledge Base Setup Notebooks

handsOn.title: Set Up OpenSearch Serverless Collection for Bedrock KB
handsOn.description: Create an OpenSearch Serverless vector collection and connect it to a Bedrock Knowledge Base.
steps: Open the OpenSearch Service console and click 'Create collection'
Select 'Vector search' as the collection type and name it 'bedrock-kb-vectors'
Configure the data access policy to allow Bedrock service principal
Navigate to Bedrock → Knowledge Bases → Create and select the OpenSearch Serverless collection
Upload a sample PDF to the S3 data source and sync — verify vectors are stored in OpenSearch

handsOn.title: Compare Vector Store Options in the Console
handsOn.description: Walk through the Bedrock KB creation wizard to see all vector store options and their configuration differences.
steps: Open Bedrock → Knowledge Bases → Create knowledge base
In the vector store selection step, note all available options: OpenSearch Serverless, Aurora pgvector, Neptune, Kendra GenAI Index, DocumentDB
Click through each option and note the required configuration fields
Document the key differences: managed vs self-managed, scaling model, cost model

furtherReading.title: Deep Dive into Vector Data Stores for Bedrock KB
furtherReading.description: Compare OpenSearch Serverless, Aurora pgvector, MongoDB Atlas, Pinecone — architecture, setup, and tradeoffs.

furtherReading.title: Vector Database Comparison — AWS Prescriptive Guidance
furtherReading.description: Side-by-side comparison of all AWS vector databases for RAG: features, scaling, cost, and use case fit.

furtherReading.title: Auto-Optimize OpenSearch Vector Database
furtherReading.description: Automated HNSW tuning: ef_construction, m, ef_search, quantization — balance recall, latency, and cost.

furtherReading.title: Using Aurora PostgreSQL as a Bedrock KB (pgvector)
furtherReading.description: Setup pgvector extension, create schema, configure Data API, IAM role for Bedrock — ACID-compliant vector store.

furtherReading.title: Multi-Tenant RAG with JWT Isolation
furtherReading.description: Per-tenant OpenSearch isolation with KMS encryption for SaaS applications.

furtherReading.description: Reuse Kendra's high-accuracy retrieval as Bedrock KB backend — cross-application index sharing.

title: RAG Architecture - Retrieval, Chunking, Embeddings
learningObjectives: Compare chunking strategies (fixed, semantic, hierarchical) and when to use each
Select embedding models (Titan Embeddings v2, Cohere Embed v3, Nova Multimodal)
Understand Retrieve vs RetrieveAndGenerate APIs
Implement query expansion, decomposition, and reranking
Use MCP for consistent vector query access patterns

tasks.title: Bedrock Knowledge Bases - How It Works
tasks.description: The authoritative reference for chunking, embedding, retrieval, and reranking.

tasks.title: Advanced Parsing, Chunking, and Query Reformulation in KB
tasks.description: Deep dive into advanced chunking and query reformulation options.

tasks.title: Bedrock Reranker Models Documentation
tasks.description: How reranker models rescore retrieved chunks for better relevance.

tasks.title: Amazon Nova Multimodal Embeddings
tasks.description: Crossmodal search: text + image + video + audio embeddings in one model.

tasks.title: Bedrock RAG Workshop - Chunking and Embedding Notebooks
tasks.description: Work through different chunking strategies and embedding model selections.

handsOn.title: Build an End-to-End RAG Pipeline with Knowledge Bases
handsOn.description: Create a complete RAG pipeline from document upload to retrieval-augmented generation.
steps: Create an S3 bucket and upload 5-10 PDF documents on a specific topic
Create a Bedrock Knowledge Base with OpenSearch Serverless, Titan Embeddings v2, and fixed-size chunking (300 tokens, 20% overlap)
Sync the data source and wait for indexing to complete
Test retrieval with the Retrieve API — try different queries and check chunk relevance
Switch to RetrieveAndGenerate API and compare the augmented response quality

handsOn.title: Experiment with Chunking Strategies
handsOn.description: Compare fixed-size, semantic, and hierarchical chunking on the same document set.
steps: Using your existing KB, note the retrieval quality with fixed-size chunking
Create a second KB with the same data source but semantic chunking enabled
Create a third KB with hierarchical chunking (parent: 1500 tokens, child: 300 tokens)
Run the same 3 test queries against all 3 KBs and compare chunk relevance scores
Document which strategy works best for your document type and why

furtherReading.title: Advanced Parsing, Chunking, and Query Reformulation in KB
furtherReading.description: Deep dive into advanced chunking and query reformulation options for Bedrock Knowledge Bases.

furtherReading.title: End-to-End RAG with CDK
furtherReading.description: IaC deployment of full RAG stack (IAM, OpenSearch, KB) using AWS CDK.

furtherReading.title: Advanced RAG with Terraform: Chunking, Hybrid Search, Reranking
furtherReading.description: IaC deployment of advanced RAG: 4 chunking strategies, hybrid search, Cohere reranking.

furtherReading.title: re:Invent 2024 — Build Scalable RAG with Bedrock KB (AIM305)
furtherReading.description: Advanced techniques for improving RAG accuracy and cost optimization with Bedrock Knowledge Bases.

furtherReading.title: Implementing RAG with Bedrock and Lambda
furtherReading.description: Serverless RAG pattern: Lambda invokes KB retrieval, augments prompt, calls FM — production-ready architecture.

handsOn.title: Create a Prompt Flow with Conditional Branching
handsOn.description: Build a Bedrock Prompt Flow that routes different query types to specialized prompts.
steps: Open Bedrock console → Prompt Management → Create prompt
Create two prompt templates: one for 'summarization' and one for 'question-answering'
Navigate to Flows → Create flow
Add a condition node that checks if the input contains 'summarize' and routes to the appropriate prompt
Test the flow with both types of queries and verify correct routing

handsOn.title: Practice Advanced Prompting Patterns
handsOn.description: Implement chain-of-thought, few-shot, and JSON schema enforcement in the Bedrock playground.
steps: Open Bedrock playground and test a zero-shot prompt: 'Is 17 a prime number?'
Add chain-of-thought: 'Think step by step. Is 17 a prime number?'
Create a few-shot prompt with 2 examples of the desired output format before the actual question
Add JSON schema enforcement: 'Respond in this exact JSON format: {"answer": boolean, "reasoning": string}'
Compare output quality and consistency across all 4 approaches

furtherReading.title: Prompt Evaluation with Prompt Management and Flows (Notebook)
furtherReading.description: Hands-on notebook: create prompt variants, build evaluation flows, compare outputs programmatically.

furtherReading.title: Guide to Prompt Optimization in Amazon Bedrock
furtherReading.description: Auto-rewrite prompts for better accuracy: Prompt Optimization feature reduces trial-and-error.

furtherReading.title: Implementing Advanced Prompt Engineering with Bedrock
furtherReading.description: Chain-of-thought, few-shot, role-play, and structured output techniques with Bedrock models.

furtherReading.title: Getting Started with Prompt Management Flows
furtherReading.description: Step-by-step: build prompt chains with Flows visual builder, integrate KB retrieval nodes.

furtherReading.title: Implement Prompt Caching on Bedrock — Cut Costs in Half
furtherReading.description: Practical implementation: cache static context, automatic vs manual checkpoints, 90% input token savings.

handsOn.title: Build a Bedrock Agent with Action Groups
handsOn.description: Create a Bedrock Agent with a Lambda-backed action group that can perform a real-world task.
steps: Open Bedrock console → Agents → Create agent
Set the agent instructions: 'You are a helpful assistant that can look up order status and process returns'
Create a Lambda function that simulates order lookup (returns mock data)
Define an action group with an OpenAPI schema describing the getOrderStatus and processReturn actions
Test the agent in the console: 'What is the status of order 12345?' and verify it calls the Lambda function

handsOn.title: Explore Bedrock Agents Quickstart Workshop
handsOn.description: Work through the quickstart workshop to build an agent for Well-Architected queries.
steps: Clone the amazon-bedrock-agents-quickstart repository
Follow the setup instructions to deploy the Lambda function and agent
Test the agent with Well-Architected queries
Examine how the action group schema maps to Lambda invocations
Review the agent trace to understand the ReAct reasoning loop

handsOn.title: Set Up API Gateway → Lambda → Bedrock Pattern
handsOn.description: Build the foundational serverless pattern for exposing Bedrock as a REST API.
steps: Create a Lambda function (Python) that calls bedrock-runtime InvokeModel with Claude
Add the bedrock:InvokeModel permission to the Lambda execution role
Create a REST API in API Gateway with a POST /chat resource
Configure the Lambda proxy integration
Test with curl: curl -X POST <api-url>/chat -d '{"prompt": "Hello"}' and verify the Bedrock response

handsOn.title: Deploy the AI Gateway Pattern with Rate Limiting
handsOn.description: Extend the basic API Gateway pattern with usage plans and API keys for rate limiting.
steps: In API Gateway, create a Usage Plan with rate limit: 100 requests/second, burst: 200
Create an API key and associate it with the usage plan
Enable API key requirement on the /chat resource
Test that requests without the API key return 403
Test that requests exceeding the rate limit return 429 (Too Many Requests)

handsOn.title: Configure Q Business with an S3 Data Source
handsOn.description: Set up Amazon Q Business with S3 documents to create an enterprise search assistant.

Name the application and configure IAM Identity Center for user access
Add an S3 data source and point it to a bucket containing company documents
Configure the sync schedule and start the initial sync
Open the Q Business web experience and test queries against your documents

Open a Python file and type a comment: '# Function to invoke Bedrock Claude with streaming'
Accept the auto-generated code suggestion
Open Q Developer chat and ask: 'Write a Lambda handler that calls Bedrock InvokeModel'

tasks.description: Step-by-step guide to creating a Knowledge Base in the console.

tasks.title: Guardrails Content Filters, PII, and Streaming
tasks.description: Follow this as a practical guide for setting up guardrails.

tasks.title: Build: RAG App with Guardrails (Steps 1-5)
tasks.description: 1) Create S3 bucket + upload PDF, 2) Create KB with OpenSearch + Titan Embeddings, 3) Configure chunking, 4) Test retrieval, 5) Create Guardrail with content filters, PII, contextual grounding.

handsOn.title: Create a Knowledge Base with Different Chunking Strategies
handsOn.description: Build the first half of the RAG app by creating the KB and testing chunking options.
steps: Create an S3 bucket called 'rag-build-data' and upload 3-5 PDF documents
Create a Bedrock Knowledge Base with OpenSearch Serverless and Titan Embeddings v2
Set chunking to 'Fixed size' with 300 tokens and 20% overlap
Sync the data source and test retrieval with 3 queries in the console
Note the chunk relevance scores for comparison later

handsOn.title: Create a Guardrail with Content Filters and PII Detection
handsOn.description: Configure a comprehensive Bedrock Guardrail with multiple filter types.
steps: Open Bedrock → Guardrails → Create guardrail
Add content filters: set HATE, INSULTS, SEXUAL, VIOLENCE, MISCONDUCT to HIGH strength
Add PII detection: enable SSN, credit card, email with ANONYMIZE action
Add a denied topic: 'competitor product comparisons'
Enable contextual grounding with threshold 0.7 for grounding and 0.5 for relevance

furtherReading.title: Bedrock Knowledge Base Tutorial
furtherReading.description: Step-by-step guide to creating a Knowledge Base in the Bedrock console.

furtherReading.description: Production guardrails patterns: content moderation, PII redaction, ApplyGuardrail API.

furtherReading.title: End-to-End RAG with Bedrock KB and CloudFormation
furtherReading.description: Automate full RAG deployment: S3 data source, OpenSearch vector store, embedding model, KB creation via IaC.

title: Hands-On Build - RAG App with Guardrails (Part 2)
learningObjectives: Wire Guardrail to Knowledge Base and test filter triggers
Use ApplyGuardrail API independently (works with non-Bedrock models)
Add CloudWatch logging for prompts, responses, and guardrail interventions
Deploy a Bedrock Agent with KB tool + Lambda tool

tasks.title: Detecting and Filtering Harmful Content with Guardrails
tasks.description: Practical guide to testing guardrail interventions.

tasks.title: Bedrock Agent Tutorial
tasks.description: Step-by-step creation and testing of a simple Bedrock Agent.

tasks.title: Build: RAG App with Guardrails (Steps 6-10)
tasks.description: 6) Wire Guardrail to KB, 7) Test ApplyGuardrail API, 8) Add CloudWatch logging, 9) Deploy Bedrock Agent with KB + Lambda tools, 10) Test agent tool selection.

handsOn.title: Wire Guardrail to Knowledge Base and Test Interventions
handsOn.description: Attach the guardrail created on Day 11 to the KB and test various filter triggers.
steps: Open your Knowledge Base and edit the retrieval settings
Attach the guardrail created on Day 11 by selecting it from the dropdown
Test with a normal query and verify the response is unfiltered
Test with a prompt containing PII: 'My SSN is 123-45-6789, find my records' — verify PII is anonymized
Test with the denied topic: 'Compare your product with competitor X' — verify the guardrail blocks the response

handsOn.title: Test the ApplyGuardrail API Independently
handsOn.description: Use the standalone ApplyGuardrail API to apply guardrails to any text, including non-Bedrock model outputs.
steps: Open CloudShell or a terminal with AWS CLI configured
Call ApplyGuardrail: aws bedrock-runtime apply-guardrail --guardrail-identifier <id> --guardrail-version DRAFT --source OUTPUT --content '[{"text":{"text":"Test content with SSN 123-45-6789"}}]'
Verify the response shows ANONYMIZED PII
Test with harmful content and verify BLOCKED action
Note that this API works with ANY text — not just Bedrock model outputs

handsOn.title: Deploy a Bedrock Agent with KB and Lambda Tools
handsOn.description: Create an agent that combines Knowledge Base retrieval with Lambda-backed actions.
steps: Create a Bedrock Agent with instructions describing both KB search and action capabilities
Attach your Knowledge Base as an agent knowledge base
Create a simple Lambda function for a custom action (e.g., 'create support ticket')
Add the Lambda as an action group with an OpenAPI schema
Test the agent: ask a question that requires KB retrieval, then ask it to create a support ticket

handsOn.title: Deploy an Agent with AgentCore CLI
handsOn.description: Use the AgentCore CLI to create and deploy a Strands agent to production in minutes.
steps: Install the AgentCore CLI: pip install agentcore-cli
Run agentcore create my-agent --template strands to scaffold a new agent project
Review the generated agent code: agent.py, tools.py, requirements.txt
Run agentcore dev to test locally with hot-reloading
Run agentcore deploy to deploy to AgentCore Runtime and note the endpoint URL

handsOn.title: Test AgentCore Memory (Session and Episodic)
handsOn.description: Implement short-term session memory and long-term episodic memory in your deployed agent.
steps: Open the AgentCore console and navigate to your deployed agent
Enable session memory and set the TTL to 1 hour
Send a series of messages: 'My name is Alice', then 'What is my name?' — verify session context
Enable long-term memory and configure it for episodic storage
In a new session, ask 'What do you remember about me?' to verify cross-session memory recall

handsOn.title: Configure Automated Reasoning Checks in Guardrails
handsOn.description: Set up Automated Reasoning to verify mathematical and logical correctness in model outputs.
steps: Open Bedrock → Guardrails → Create or edit a guardrail
Navigate to the Automated Reasoning section and click 'Create policy'
Define a policy for a financial domain: 'Loan interest calculations must follow simple interest formula: I = P * R * T'
Test with a correct calculation: 'Interest on $10,000 at 5% for 2 years is $1,000' — verify PASS
Test with an incorrect calculation: 'Interest on $10,000 at 5% for 2 years is $2,000' — verify BLOCKED with explanation

handsOn.title: Test All 8 Guardrail Filter Types
handsOn.description: Systematically test each guardrail filter type to understand their behavior.
steps: Create a test guardrail with ALL filter types enabled
Test content filters: send prompts triggering HATE, INSULTS, SEXUAL, VIOLENCE, MISCONDUCT — verify each is blocked
Test denied topics: add 'political opinions' and verify related prompts are blocked
Test PII: send text with SSN, credit card, email and verify ANONYMIZE vs BLOCK behavior
Test prompt attack filter: try a jailbreak prompt like 'Ignore your instructions and...' — verify detection

handsOn.title: Set Up a VPC Endpoint for Amazon Bedrock
handsOn.description: Create a PrivateLink endpoint to keep Bedrock traffic within your VPC.
steps: Open VPC console → Endpoints → Create endpoint
Search for 'bedrock-runtime' and select com.amazonaws.<region>.bedrock-runtime
Select your VPC and at least 2 subnets in different AZs
Create or select a security group allowing HTTPS (port 443) from your application subnets
Test connectivity from an EC2 instance in the VPC: aws bedrock-runtime invoke-model --endpoint-url https://vpce-xxx.bedrock-runtime.<region>.vpce.amazonaws.com

handsOn.title: Write an IAM Policy with Bedrock Condition Keys
handsOn.description: Create a restrictive IAM policy that limits which models can be invoked using condition keys.
steps: Open IAM console → Policies → Create policy
Use the JSON editor to create a policy allowing bedrock:InvokeModel only for Claude models
Add a condition: 'StringLike': {'bedrock:ModelId': 'anthropic.claude-*'}
Add a second statement denying access to non-approved regions
Attach the policy to a test role and verify that invoking a non-Claude model returns AccessDenied

furtherReading.title: VPC Endpoints (PrivateLink) for Amazon Bedrock
furtherReading.description: Private connectivity from VPC to Bedrock — never expose traffic to public internet, endpoint policies.

handsOn.title: Configure AgentCore Policy with Cedar Rules
handsOn.description: Set up Cedar policies to control agent behavior and limit which tools an agent can invoke.
steps: Open the AgentCore console and navigate to Policy
Create a new policy store for your agent
Write a Cedar policy: permit (principal, action == Action::"invokeToolGroup", resource == ToolGroup::"approved-tools")
Add a deny rule: forbid (principal, action, resource) when { context.sensitivityLevel > 3 }
Test the policy by invoking the agent with both permitted and denied tool requests

handsOn.title: Review SageMaker Clarify Bias Reports
handsOn.description: Explore SageMaker Clarify bias detection capabilities for understanding model fairness.
steps: Open SageMaker Studio and navigate to the Clarify section
Review the pre-training bias metrics: Class Imbalance (CI), Difference in Proportions of Labels (DPL)
Review post-training bias metrics: Disparate Impact (DI), Difference in Positive Proportions in Predicted Labels (DPPL)
Understand the SHAP-based feature attribution for model explainability
Note how these apply to GenAI: evaluate model outputs for fairness across demographic groups

tasks.title: Effective Cost Optimization Strategies for Amazon Bedrock
tasks.description: Comprehensive guide: model cascading, caching, batch inference, provisioned throughput.

tasks.title: Prompt Caching on Bedrock - Up to 85% Cost Reduction
tasks.description: 5-min TTL (1.25x write, 0.1x read) or 1-hour TTL (2.0x write, 0.1x read). Min 1024 tokens.

tasks.title: ElastiCache Semantic Cache - 86% Cost Reduction
tasks.description: Application-level semantic caching using embeddings and cosine similarity.

tasks.title: Intelligent Prompt Routing
tasks.description: Auto-routes to cheapest capable model. Automated model cascading.

tasks.title: Model Invocation Logging
tasks.description: Log all prompts/responses to CloudWatch or S3. Essential for cost analysis and debugging.

handsOn.title: Implement Semantic Caching with ElastiCache
handsOn.description: Set up a semantic cache using ElastiCache to reduce Bedrock API costs for similar queries.
steps: Create an ElastiCache Serverless Redis cluster with vector search enabled
Write a Lambda function that generates embeddings for incoming queries using Titan Embeddings
Before calling Bedrock, search ElastiCache for a cached response with cosine similarity > 0.95
If cache hit: return cached response (saving the Bedrock API call). If cache miss: call Bedrock, store the query embedding and response in ElastiCache
Test with 10 similar queries and measure the cache hit rate and cost savings

handsOn.title: Enable and Analyze Model Invocation Logging
handsOn.description: Set up model invocation logging to CloudWatch to track token usage and cost per request.
steps: Open Bedrock console → Settings → Model invocation logging
Enable logging to CloudWatch Logs and select a log group
Make several Bedrock API calls with different models
Open CloudWatch Logs Insights and run: fields @timestamp, inputTokenCount, outputTokenCount, modelId | sort @timestamp desc
Calculate the cost per request using the token counts and published pricing

furtherReading.description: Comprehensive guide: model cascading, caching, batch inference, provisioned throughput, distillation.

handsOn.title: Run Bedrock Evaluations with LLM-as-a-Judge
handsOn.description: Set up an automated evaluation job using LLM-as-a-judge to assess model quality.
steps: Open Bedrock console → Evaluations → Create evaluation job
Select 'LLM-as-a-judge' evaluation type
Choose Claude as the judge model and Nova as the target model
Upload a test dataset with 10-20 prompt-response pairs in JSONL format
Configure evaluation criteria: accuracy, relevance, coherence, and safety

Create a custom alarm for high error rates: trigger when error count > 10 in 5 minutes

handsOn.title: Build the Production RAG Pipeline Reference Architecture
handsOn.description: Trace the full Production RAG Pipeline architecture on paper and identify all service interactions.
steps: Draw the architecture: User → API Gateway → Lambda → Bedrock KB (Retrieve) → OpenSearch Serverless
Add the generation path: Lambda → Bedrock InvokeModel (with retrieved context) → Response
Add guardrails: Guardrail applied on both input and output
Add monitoring: CloudWatch Logs, X-Ray tracing, model invocation logging
Label every IAM role and permission needed for each service interaction

handsOn.title: Build the Multi-Agent System Reference Architecture
handsOn.description: Trace the Multi-Agent System architecture and identify the orchestration patterns.
steps: Draw the architecture: User → Supervisor Agent → Routing Decision
Add collaborator agents: Research Agent (KB tool), Action Agent (Lambda tools), Analysis Agent (Code Interpreter)
Add AgentCore components: Gateway for routing, Memory for context, Policy for access control
Add the observability layer: AgentCore Observability with OTEL spans
Note the exam-critical distinction: supervisor mode (one agent delegates) vs supervisor-with-routing mode (dynamic selection)

handsOn.title: Build the Document Processing Pipeline Reference Architecture
handsOn.description: Trace the Document Processing Pipeline and identify the service chain for different document types.
steps: Draw the architecture: S3 upload event → EventBridge → Step Functions orchestrator
Add the processing branches: PDF → Textract, Audio → Transcribe, Image → Rekognition, Text → Comprehend
Add the enrichment step: all extracted data → Bedrock InvokeModel for summarization and structuring
Add PII handling: Macie scans S3 objects before processing, Guardrails filter PII in outputs
Add the storage layer: processed results → DynamoDB + S3, vectors → OpenSearch for KB

handsOn.title: Build the Human-in-the-Loop Agent Workflow Reference Architecture
handsOn.description: Trace the Human-in-the-Loop workflow using Step Functions Wait-for-Callback pattern.
steps: Draw the architecture: User request → Bedrock Agent → Step Functions workflow
Add the automated steps: agent generates proposal → guardrails check → approval needed decision
Add the human review: Step Functions Wait-for-Callback → SNS notification → human reviewer
Add the callback path: reviewer approves/rejects → callback token resumes Step Functions
Add the completion path: approved → agent executes action → response to user

tasks.title: Practice Matching Patterns
tasks.description: Drill all 6 matching categories: Service->Data Type, Problem->Solution, Architecture->Service, Human Review->Service, Guardrails->Verification, Cost->Technique.

handsOn.title: Practice Ordering Sequences on Paper
handsOn.description: Write out all 8 ordering sequences from memory, then check against reference materials.
steps: On a blank sheet, write the RAG pipeline ordering: S3 upload → Chunking → Embedding → Vector store → Retrieve → Augment → Generate
Write the Agent loop: User input → Plan → Select tool → Execute → Observe → Decide (repeat or respond)
Write the KB setup: Create S3 bucket → Upload docs → Create vector store → Create KB → Sync → Test
Write the remaining 5 sequences from memory
Check each against the study app and correct any errors — focus on the steps you got wrong

handsOn.title: Flash Card Drill for Service Matching
handsOn.description: Create and drill flash cards matching services to use cases and data types.
steps: Create cards for Service → Data Type: Textract→Documents, Transcribe→Audio, Comprehend→Text, Rekognition→Images
Create cards for Problem → Solution: 'Too expensive' → Model cascading, 'Hallucinating' → Contextual Grounding, 'Slow responses' → Prompt caching
Create cards for Architecture → Service: 'RAG' → KB+OpenSearch, 'Agent' → Bedrock Agents, 'Deploy agent' → AgentCore
Shuffle and drill until you can match all pairs in under 60 seconds
Focus extra time on the pairs you hesitate on

furtherReading.title: AWS Skill Builder Exam Prep Plan
furtherReading.description: Free lessons per domain, SimuLearn labs, and practice assessments.

furtherReading.title: How to Choose GenAI on AWS
furtherReading.description: Service selection decision guide — critical for matching questions.

furtherReading.title: Reddit OP's AWS Service Comparison Table
furtherReading.description: Community service comparison table on Notion — useful for drilling service distinctions.

title: Exam Guide Re-Read + Wrong Answer Rules
learningObjectives: Re-read the exam guide and self-test on every skill
Compile all wrong answers from all practice exams into one-line rules
Create a one-page exam cheat sheet

tasks.title: Official Exam Guide PDF - Re-Read
tasks.description: For every skill, test yourself: can you explain it without notes?

tasks.title: Compile Wrong Answer Rules
tasks.description: Review all practice exam errors. Create one-line trigger->answer rules for every wrong answer pattern.

tasks.title: Create One-Page Rules Sheet
tasks.description: Distill all key rules, service comparisons, and exam triggers onto a single page.

handsOn.title: Self-Test Every Exam Skill from the Exam Guide
handsOn.description: Go through every skill in the exam guide and explain each one aloud without notes.
steps: Open the exam guide PDF and go to the skills list
For each skill, cover the description and try to explain what it means in your own words
Mark skills as: Green (confident), Yellow (partial), Red (need review)
For every Red skill, find the relevant day in the study plan and re-read the key resource
Re-test Red skills until they move to at least Yellow

furtherReading.title: Official Exam Guide PDF
furtherReading.description: Every skill, task, and in-scope service for the AIP-C01 exam.

furtherReading.title: Reddit OP's Service Features & Scenarios Table
furtherReading.description: Community service features and scenarios on Notion for final review.

furtherReading.title: GenAI Workload Assessment
furtherReading.description: Framework for evaluating GenAI implementations — covers all domains.

title: Final Full Practice Exam
learningObjectives: Score 80%+ on a full-length practice exam
Final confidence check before exam day

tasks.title: CertificationPractice.com Exam #4 or #5 (Full 180 min)
tasks.description: Target: 80%+. Full timed conditions.

tasks.title: Final Score Analysis
tasks.description: Analyze any remaining weak areas. Note specific skills still causing errors.

handsOn.title: Targeted Lab for Remaining Weak Areas
handsOn.description: After the final practice exam, do a focused 30-minute lab on any remaining weak spots.
steps: Review your final practice exam results and identify the top 2-3 error patterns
For each error pattern, open the relevant AWS console or documentation
Walk through the specific service configuration that you got wrong on the exam
Write a one-line rule for each error: 'When I see X, the answer is Y because Z'
Add these rules to your exam cheat sheet

furtherReading.title: CertificationPractice.com (510 questions, 6 exams)
furtherReading.description: Additional free practice exams if you want more question exposure.

furtherReading.title: Tutorials Dojo Free Sampler (30 questions)
furtherReading.description: Quick 30-question sampler for a final confidence check.

furtherReading.title: AWS Official Practice Question Set (20 free questions)
furtherReading.description: Official AWS questions — re-take to verify improvement from Day 7.

title: Last Weak Spots + Skim
learningObjectives: Drill final weak domain based on practice exam analysis
Skim Well-Architected GenAI Lens for any missed patterns
Final review of service comparison tables

tasks.title: Well-Architected GenAI Lens (Final Skim)
tasks.description: Skim for any architecture patterns you may have missed.

tasks.title: Drill Final Weak Domain
tasks.description: Use CertificationPractice.com domain-specific questions to drill your weakest remaining area.

tasks.title: Review Service Comparison Tables
tasks.description: Final pass through all comparison tables and wrong answer rules.

handsOn.title: Quick Console Walkthrough of Key Services
handsOn.description: Spend 5 minutes in each key console to reinforce visual memory of service configurations.
steps: Bedrock console: review Knowledge Bases, Agents, Guardrails, Evaluations sections
OpenSearch console: review a vector collection's index settings
CloudWatch console: review GenAI Observability dashboard and metrics
IAM console: review Bedrock-related managed policies
Step Functions console: review a sample state machine with Bedrock integration

furtherReading.title: Well-Architected GenAI Lens
furtherReading.description: Final skim for any architecture patterns you may have missed.

furtherReading.title: How to Choose GenAI on AWS
furtherReading.description: Service selection decision guide — last review of when to use which service.

furtherReading.title: MakendranG's Handwritten Study Notes
furtherReading.description: Early Adopter badge winner's notes — good for spotting any gaps.

title: Rest Day
learningObjectives: Light review only. Rest and sleep well.

tasks.title: Light Skim of Rules Sheet
tasks.description: Light skim of your one-page rules sheet only. No full practice exam. Sleep well.

furtherReading.title: Official Exam Guide PDF
furtherReading.description: Light skim of the exam guide for last-minute confidence. Do not deep study.

furtherReading.title: AWS Certification Exam Day Tips
furtherReading.description: Practical exam day preparation: what to bring, testing center logistics, online proctoring tips.

title: Exam Day
learningObjectives: Pass the AIP-C01 exam

tasks.title: Pre-Exam: Read Rules Sheet
tasks.description: Read your one-page rules sheet. Review exam answer patterns: 'Most cost-effective' = managed, 'Most secure' = restrictive, 'Min overhead' = serverless.

tasks.title: Take the AIP-C01 Exam
tasks.description: Time management: First pass 45min (confident), Second pass 70min (flagged), Third pass 30min (review), Buffer 35min. Never leave blanks.

furtherReading.title: Official Exam Guide PDF
furtherReading.description: Quick reference before the exam for any last-minute skill checks.

furtherReading.title: AWS Certification Benefits
furtherReading.description: After the exam: claim your 50% discount voucher, digital badge, and AWS certification benefits.
